response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong software craftsmanship and has successfully worked on complex systems, he lacks the core technical requirements for this role. His Java expertise is listed but not demonstrated, there is no Kotlin experience, no Spring ecosystem background, and no significant IDE tooling experience beyond 6 months at National Instruments a decade ago. His recent 5-year trajectory (TypeScript/Node.js/full-stack) has moved away from the JVM ecosystem this role requires, and there's no evidence of the enterprise Java framework depth needed for Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate work.
strengths: Proven ability to design and maintain complex, large-scale systems (Dunnhumby micro-services infrastructure, Kubernetes-deployed GIS platform, real-time data handling)
Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, clean architecture, and CI/CD implementation demonstrated across multiple roles
Excellent communication and teaching skills evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and community contributions
concerns: No demonstrated expertise in Java or Kotlin despite both being listed; no mention of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or other enterprise JVM frameworks critical to the role
Limited and dated IDE/language tooling experience (6-month National Instruments role in 2014-2015); career trajectory has moved toward web/full-stack development away from JVM ecosystem
Generalist engineer across web, mobile, embedded, and GIS domains rather than specialized depth in enterprise Java development and IDE internals
